entry.title: Re: Warning "Data has been changed in parallel session" w...
entry.content:
Hello @t_jungesblut 

As the note 3440905 - Warning "Data has been changed in parallel session" when save Freight Order that is a standard functionality. Thus it is not an issue to be resolved. That is simply how deferred processing in TM works.

If you don't like it, you can minimize the effect by not using creation/change/save strategies. They are customize in on freight order type level - SPRO: Transportation Management -> Freight Order Management -> Freight Order -> Define Freight Order Types.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Become a Certified SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Project Manager
entry.content:
In the dynamic world of digital transformation, where businesses are constantly evolving to stay competitive, project management is more crucial than ever. Central to this landscape is the role of professionals equipped to manage complex cloud projects. With the newly launched SAP Certification for Managing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Projects (C_S4PM), you can now become a certified project manager and take charge of leading transformative initiatives.

Designed for Project Managers, Consultants, and Technical Leads, this certification empowers you to excel in managing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition implementations, positioning you as a key player in your organization's digital transformation projects with strategic guidance and expert integration.

How can you prepare for this certification?

You can prepare for the exam in your own pace, by completing the "Managing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Projects" learning journey, designed to provide comprehensive insight into SAP S/4HANA Cloud project management. With free access to the learning journey, you can deepen your expertise of managing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition implementations, specifically focusing on SAP tools, methodologies, and SAP Business Suite portfolio.

Additionally, SAP Community blog posts provide some supporting information to deepen your understanding and preparation:

  • "Guiding Your Cloud Journey: The New SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Adoption Plan Roadmap"
  • "SAP Partner: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Implementation Tools Overview"
  • "News from SAP Activate - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 and more"

You can also explore more insights about SAP Activate for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition on SAP for Me and taking a look at the release notes.

As a prerequisite, candidates should possess project management experience, preferred in ERP implementation, especially SAP Cloud-related projects. Additionally, hands-on experience with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition solutions and experience with managing cloud-based SAP projects is beneficial.

Kickstart your journey today

By becoming a certified project manager for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, you will be able to position yourself as a key contributor to your organization's growth and innovation. You can demonstrate a high level of competency in orchestrating SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition projects, showcasing expertise and leadership in cloud initiatives that are aligned with business objectives.

Certified learners need to continuously keep their skills up-to-date and certifications valid by completing mandatory assessments before their certification expires. With an SAP Learning Hub subscription, you can prepare for and take the stay certified assessments at any time during the validity period of your certification.  If you want to know more on how to get and stay certified, you can read this blog post.

Open new professional opportunities for you with new learning and a sought-after certification. Build the skills you need on the SAP Learning site and get inspired by reading learner stories on SAP Community, ask questions and connect with fellow learners. When you’re ready, take your certification exam and start shaping your industry's future!

entry.title: COMING SOON! Product Compliance Processes in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION:

Topics in this Academic Lesson include: regional compliance regulations, the costs of non-compliance, the impact of product compliance issues on business processes, product compliance throughout the value chain, and systems controls related to compliance for the organization, its customers and its suppliers. 

The first Activity of this Academic Lesson provides a comprehensive explanation of the various facets of product compliance management; such as, chemical compliance, dangerous goods management, data sheets and documentation, and product compliance-related analytics. The second Activity provides students with the opportunity to complete product compliance tasks directly in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. 

SUGGESTED AREAS OF STUDY:

Business, product design and development, engineering, manufacturing and production, transportation management, data management, and studies relevant to oil, gas, and chemical industries.

Appropriate for students at undergraduate and graduate level university.  

DOWNLOAD:

Microsoft Word editable format: coming soon

PDF non-editable format:  coming soon

entry.title: Re: Upload General Journal Entry - Incomplete documents
entry.content:
Hello again dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

When using the "Upload General Journal Entries" app in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with the standard template, the system will post the valid entries from the file and skip any that contain errors. This partial posting is actually the expected system behavior, and unfortunately, there's no built-in setting to make the upload act like an “all-or-nothing” process (where nothing gets posted if any entry is wrong).

Here’s a practical workaround :

Use the "Import General Journal Entries" app instead

This app works a bit differently and gives you more control. You can upload the file, simulate the entries first to catch any issues, and only post the file when everything looks good. That way, you can make sure nothing gets posted unless the entire file is error-free.

I hope this will help you answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: Why is the COGS Third Party 5030200 standard YCOA acc...
entry.content:
Hello again dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

SAP sets the 5030200 COGS Third Party account and most 5* accounts  as not tax relevant by default because these accounts are used for internal cost reporting, not for transactions where VAT is actually calculated.

Tax is handled when you post an invoice (e.g., a vendor invoice for goods), not when the cost hits the COGS account. COGS is usually updated automatically after goods are delivered, so there's no tax to post again at that point.

Marking these accounts as non-tax relevant helps prevent mistakes, keeps the tax logic clean and compliant, and ensures tax is only posted where it’s legally required like on sales or purchase transactions.

I hope this will help you answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: Build and Run Multi-Tenant SaaS Solutions on the SAP BTP
entry.content:
Update May 2025

The May-update of the Partner Reference Application “Poetry Slam Manager” addresses application management topics as well as architecture enhancements:

  • The ability to adopt customer specific expectations in multitenant standard solutions is key for market success. The chapter Enable Key User Flexibility Using SAP Build Work Zone provides you all information at hand to empower your customers to customize the UIs of multitenant applications to meet company-specific preferences. 
  • We added the SAP Job Scheduling Service to schedule and trigger jobs for background execution in chapter Schedule Jobs and Add Background Execution. The example provides a reference architecture to benefit from background jobs as partner application manager, as well as customer business user.

  • The tutorial SAP Business One Integration Using a Cloud Connector describes how to establish a secure connection between SAP BTP-based applications and on-premises solutions such as SAP Business One and SAP S/4HANA using the SAP Connectivity Service and the SAP Cloud Connector.
  • For application managers we added tutorials and configuration examples to upgrade multitenant SaaS applications and CI/CD pipelines using the SAP Continuous Integration and Delivery Service.

Our next stop to meet you in person is the Code Connect event re>≡CAP 2025 on July 9th at the SAP University in St. Leon-Rot, Germany. Looking forward to meet you there! 😊

entry.title: Re: Issue with multiple SAP Commerce Composable Storefron...
entry.content:
As mentioned in the documentation and re-iterated in KBA, in order to access your B2C storefront you need to create and use an endpoint with the following format: 

mystore.<domain-name>.<ext>

notice the desired application name as sub-domain.

If you want to use custom domain names you need to map each jsapps with its endpoint using properties, for example:

jsapps.mystore.servername.0=<b2c-endpoint-domain>   // to access b2c storefront
jsapps.b2bstore.servername.0=<b2b-endpoint-domain>. // to access b2b storefront

The properties can be added from Cloud Portal as mentioned here.

entry.title: Budget Design Process for Salary Increases: a functional overview
entry.content:
A special Thank You to the many contacts across the board at SAP who made this blog possible by connecting with us and providing insights on leading market practices.

 

Introduction

The reason for this blog comes from the dreaded comment/requirement by customers to Compensation implementation partners in workshops : "We understand your leading practice of loading a budget % based on attributes (for example: attribute 1=country / attribute 2=state) but all we get from Finance each year is a maximum amount to be spent so we'd like to be able to assign a flat amount to each planner worksheet". In this blog we try to give tools to understand the bottom-up logic that facilitates the creation of budget percentages based on attributes that stick to SAP SuccessFactors leading practices (see budget section).

 

Walkthrough of the Budget Design Process before it gets loaded as a % in SAP SuccessFactors Compensation Module.

With the example of a January salary review cycle start.

 

The budget design process for salary increases involves different approaches across countries and roles. While some organizations apply a flat dollar increase to all employees across the board (sometimes as a result of Union negociations for example), most companies use a percentage-based or bottom-up approach, breaking down the overall budget into percentages by attributes based on projected needs. This methods allows for more dynamic updates during the cycle as well as more flexibility in terms of "Reward to Retain" aka retaining key talents.

 

Projected Salary Increase for upcoming cycle

  • Projections are informed by external surveys (e.g., Mercer, Aon, Towers Watson).
  • These surveys are typically free if companies participate.

 

Differentiation by Role

  • Budgeting distinguishes between professional roles vs. management.
  • Executive compensation is often analyzed separately from all other employees.

 

Country-Level Variations

  • Budgeting is localized to address country-specific rules and labor conditions.
  • For example:
    • Belgium: Annual base salary increases are mandated by the government and deducted from the country’s salary budget.
    • Austria and UK: Legal frameworks such as minimum wage laws dictate baseline adjustments.
    • UK: Adjustments are based on national minimum pay.

 

Legal and Mandatory Increases

  • These are typically mandated by:
    • Union agreements
    • Government regulations
  • These increases are generally provided as percentages.

 

Managerial Discretion & Special Adjustments

  • HR Teams may adjust salaries based on team needs or specific market conditions.
  • Special adjustments (e.g., retention, market corrections) are budgeted separately and tailored by country.

 

Workforce Changes & Their Impact

  • Headcount shifts due to employee turnover impact increase percentages and must be factored in.
  • Companies must also consider part-time transitions and fluctuating employment statuses.

 

Conclusion

  • Country-specific budget percentages are refined several times before January using specialized compensation surveys and legal constraint
  • Finance teams provide investment volumes, which are then translated into controllable country-level rates.

 

 

 

Additional Resources:

  • Compensation process diagram on the SAP Digital Library

 

 

 

 

 

All the best,

Xavier

 

(If you found this blog useful please consider giving it kudos)

entry.title: Re: M-series Apple Chip MacBooks and Abap Platform Trial containers using Docker and Podman
entry.content:
@TomHoepping good that it has started working on Orbstack as well. From the timing of things starting to work, we could make the guess, that the "fix" was not somewhere in e.g. Docker Engine, or in Orbstack, but instead occurred in the Apple Virtualisation framework via some update, for example in macOS 15.4.1 ... for us, as consumers of an amd64 container, the main thing is that it's nice there is this Rosetta-based way of running the container.

As noted somewhere above, Apple doesn't guarantee there will always be a Rosetta-solution available, but let's hope it is there for another 20 years!

entry.title: Re: Access SAP IBP Master & Key Figures data using OData Integration
entry.content:
Please be aware that there are two newer OData services with more functionality to export and also import key figures and master data:

Integrating Data with External Systems | SAP Help Portal

Integrating Master Data Using OData Service | SAP Help Portal

The first one is also supporting delta export of key figures, but only in an asynchronous process. I'm not sure if this is working in combination with Datasphere. 

Similarly updating data in IBP is an asynchronous process.

Additionally we are working on a direct integration of key figures and master data from IBP to Datasphere, which probably will have a significantly improved performance compared to the OData approach.

Best regards, Reinhard

entry.title: Disable Windows Authentication on Data Source in RPT File Via .Net
entry.content:
We have a customized version of crystal web viewer that seems to run on CRRuntime_64bit_13_0_21 for .net framework.  We have a problem where the web viewer cannot handle Integrated Security or Trusted_Connection.  We have customers that encounter logon errors when they try to use report that they created in Crystal Designer 2016 with those options enabled.   We're able to resolve this by updating the data source in Crystal Designer 2016 using Set Datasource Location.  We're looking for a way to update a larger number of rpt files quickly.

We've tried many different ways using powershell and .net.  We can update the server name, database name, user, and ODBC Type.  However, we've been unsuccessful trying to disable the options for win auth.   Most recently, I installed the .net sdk for VS 2023.  I copied the sample code for c# and adapted it for use in powershell.  Sample code found on topic 'To Log on to a Secure SQL Server Database with the ReportDocument object model'.  The updated code is shown below.

The updated code completes without errors and does save a new rpt file.  However, the options for Integrated Security and Trusted Connection still show as enabled when viewing Properties in Set Datasource Location screen in Crystal Designer 2016.

Do you think this is the best approach for solving this problem (updating the settings directly) ?   We do have standard System DSNs on our servers used to connect to our app database.  I wonder if we should be trying to connect to those instead.   

I found settings in $report.DataSourceConnections.logonproperties that don't seem to change.  Seems weird to me though because we are able to set other options (server, db, user) there.

I also found another post on this site saying exporting the report document to a rpt file may work, but i tried that and still have the same problem.  A few other posts tend to send me to a KBA that does describe my symptoms, logon info not saving to rpt file.  That says use the RAS SDK.  I don't have a RAS that I know of, just Crystal Designer and Runtime.   I'm not a dev for the custom web viewer so I'm not able to change that code and makes me think I can't change these settings at runtime.  I assume I need to update these rpt files.

# Loading Crystal Reports assemblies
[reflection.assembly]::LoadWithPartialName('CrystalDecisions.Shared') | fl
[reflection.assembly]::LoadWithPartialName('CrystalDecisions.CrystalReports.Engine') | fl

# Create a new report document
$report = New-Object CrystalDecisions.CrystalReports.Engine.ReportDocument

# Load the report
$report.Load("G:\OrigReport.rpt")

    $connectionInfo = New-Object CrystalDecisions.Shared.ConnectionInfo;
    $connectionInfo.DatabaseName = 'MyDB';
    $connectionInfo.UserID = 'Myuser';
    $connectionInfo.Password = 'Mypass';
    $connectionInfo.IntegratedSecurity = $false;

    $tables = $report.Database.Tables;
    foreach ($table in $tables)
    {
        $newLogonInfo = $table.LogOnInfo;
        $newLogonInfo.ConnectionInfo = $connectionInfo;
        $table.ApplyLogOnInfo($newLogonInfo);
    }


# Save the report if needed
$report.SaveAs("G:\ModifiedReport2.rpt")

$report.close()
$report.dispose()

We also tried using SetConnection

# Load the report
$report.Load('G:\OrigReport.rpt')

# Access the DataSourceConnections
$connections = $report.DataSourceConnections

# Modify connection properties for SQL authentication
foreach ($connection in $connections) {
    
    # Access and modify the LogonProperties
    $logonProperties = $connection.LogonProperties
    $logonProperties.Set("Integrated Security", $false)
    $logonProperties.Set("Trusted_Connection", $false)

    $Connection.SetLogonProperties($logonProperties)

    # Set the connection properties
    $connection.SetConnection("MyServer", "MYDB", "YourSQLUserID", "YourSQLPassword")

    $logonProperties = $connection.LogonProperties
}

 

We also tried export the report

# export report as rpt
$exportOptions = $report.ExportOptions
$exportOptions.ExportFormatType = [CrystalDecisions.Shared.ExportFormatType]::CrystalReport
$exportOptions.ExportDestinationType = [CrystalDecisions.Shared.ExportDestinationType]::DiskFile

# Set the destination options for disk file
$diskFileDestinationOptions = New-Object CrystalDecisions.Shared.DiskFileDestinationOptions
$diskFileDestinationOptions.DiskFileName = 'G:\ModifiedReport.rpt'
$exportOptions.DestinationOptions = $diskFileDestinationOptions

$exportOptions.FormatOptions = $null
$report.Export()

entry.title: Re: Deleting Test Plans from SAP Cloud ALM with Project L...
entry.content:
Hello Santosh,

I tried this on our dummy environment and I was able to delete with following steps:

  1. I unassigned all Test Cases within the Test Plan.
  2. I switched the status of the Test Plan from "In Testing" to "In Preparation".
  3. Then "Delete" button was visible/actionable.

Kind regards,

Gizem

entry.title: Re: Amount field length extension (AFLE) & Limitations in SAP S/4 HANA
entry.content:
Hi, is there any workaround for not having Amount field length extensión is SAP S/4 Hana Cloud Public?

We´ve placed a SAP Note and they confirmed us that currently there is no plan to make it available.
The proposed solutions from SAP are not sufficient:
-You can divide the value into several line items, having more than one line for the same material
-You can divide the value and you can use more than one condition with half of the value
-You can use different unit of measure (thus reducing the value that has to be entered)
-You can use a different currency which does not demand as many digits

We are from Argentina (inflation adjustment problems) and we can´t propose SAP S/4 Hana Cloud Public to some customers if we can´t offer a solution for this problem.

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Complete Table and Method to attain Table Number for...
entry.content:
Hi there,
To answer your question se16n contains tables having the field values present in the T code for eg: when we run the transaction code (MM01) i.e to create material the field values, we enter get stored in the form of table in  Transaction se16n.
So instead of finding the T codes in Se16n,find the field values once you enter the t code as said in the above example, and field values with respect to its table can be easily searched online.

Hope this answers your question, please do let me know if you have any questions or any suggestions.

 

Regards,

Sarvesh

entry.title: Financial Closing Cockpit pricing w/ RISE
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts,

 

In a past work experience I worked on a project integrating Blackline with SAP ECC and S4 HANA (RISE) for account reconciliation. I'm trying to understand the licensing cost for SAP's Financial Closing Cockpit. 
I've done my own research but am not finding much information on the cost range for including the FCC with RISE.

The assumptions are an accounting, audit and support team totaling 15 users with 2 special users, 10 productivity users, 2 advanced users, 2 core users and 1 self-service user. For FI org elements and master data, you have 1 Controlling Area, < 5 legal entities and ~ 200 B/S accounts to reconcile.

 

Any help would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks,

Ben

entry.title: SAP SucessFactors - Global Benefits - Other Benefits Not Displaying Data
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm having an issue where the "Other Available Benefits" section in SAP SuccessFactors does not display any data, even though I have created a benefit with the type set to "Other". 

This benefit is meant to grant employees a day off on their birthday, if it's on a working day.

  • The benefit is active and applicable to all legal entities.

  • Permissions are correctly assigned via RBP.

  • The block is added to the People Profile.

  • I’ve also tested with proxy access and different users, but no data shows up.

Has anyone encountered this issue before or knows what else I could check?

Thank you.

entry.title: Email Not Received When WebI Report Is Scheduled in Email Body (BO 4.3 SP3 Patch 3)
entry.content:
Hello all,

We are currently using SAP BusinessObjects BI 4.3 SP3 Patch 3 (version 14.3.3.4562.3). We've encountered an issue with report scheduling:

When we schedule a Web Intelligence (WebI) report to be sent as an attachment (e.g., PDF or Excel), the email is received successfully.
However, when we schedule the same report with the option to embed the report content in the email body, the schedule completes successfully, but no email is received at all.

Has anyone else experienced this behavior in BO 4.3 SP3? Are there any known fixes, workarounds, or patch updates that address this issue?

Any guidance would be appreciated.

Thank you!

entry.title: Introducing the new Terraform Provider for SAP Cloud Identity Services – Now in Beta!
entry.content:
We’re excited to announce the beta release of the Terraform provider for SAP Cloud Identity Services. Built based on customer feedback and feature requests, this provider is a much-awaited step forward in simplifying identity management.

Why Automate SAP Cloud Identity Services?

 It provides a secure, centralised approach to identity and access management across systems, making it an important part of any modern SAP landscape.

As your organisation grows, scaling identity & access management starts to become a challenge, and automation becomes essential.

With this new Terraform provider, you can bring infrastructure-as-code (IaC) practices to your identity environment, delivering:

  • Repeatability

  • Auditability

  • Governance

  • Faster deployments

What’s in the Box? 🚀

The provider will be developed in a phased and scoped manner. It will evolve based on feedback, requirements, and user scenarios.

In this beta release, our focus is on foundational resources that are central to identity & authentication:

Application

Applications represent consumers of Identity Authentication such as SAP cloud solutions, third-party apps, SAP BTP subaccount, etc.

  • Terraform Resource: Configure identity settings for applications.

  • Data Sources: Retrieve details of specific applications or list all applications within a tenant.
    📘View Docs

Group

Groups help organise users based on roles, permissions, or other criteria.

  • Terraform Resource: Create and manage user groups.

  • Data Sources: Fetch details of individual groups or get a complete list.
    📘View Docs

User

Programmatically manage end users with the ability to automate user lifecycle operations.

  • Terraform Resource: Define and manage end users.

  • Data Sources: Query individual users or list all users in a tenant.
    📘View Docs

Schema

Need custom attributes for your users? Define and manage user schemas to match your organisational needs.

  • Terraform Resource: Configure custom user schemas.

  • Data Sources: Retrieve details or lists of schemas.
    📘View Docs

The provider is open-source under the Apache 2.0 license, and powered by the public APIs published on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

A Quick Word on Beta 

This is a beta release, which means it’s an early version, intended for exploration, experimentation, and feedback.

Important Notes:

  • Do not use it in production just yet.

  • This release is for trial and evaluation only.

  • Definitions, behaviour, and features may change in future releases based on:

    • Community feedback

    • API limitations or updates

    • Real-world use cases

    • Bugs or usability issues

We Want Your Feedback!

As you test it out, let us know:

  • ✅ What works well?

  • 🐞 What’s broken?

  • 💡 What features or resources would you like to see next?

  • 🔁 What automation scenarios are you trying to solve?

You can file issues, open feature requests, or share scenarios and feedback directly on the provider’s GitHub repository. We’ll do our best to address them.

Where to Get It?

The provider is now available on the Terraform & OpenTofu registries:

We’re thrilled to take this step forward in supporting SAP Cloud Identity Services automation and can’t wait to see what the community builds with it.

🧪 Try it out.
🛠 Break things.
💬 Tell us what you need.

Let’s build something awesome together!

entry.title: How do I select which ID origin personalizes a content studio email link
entry.content:
Hi SAP Experts,

We have multiple ID Origins (i.e., email, BuPa ID, hashed email, etc.). Within Content Studio, I need to personalize a specific URL. The use case is to appended & personalize the URL with the BuPa ID for the respective email recipient.

Example: my list contains two recipients; myself and a colleague. My BuPa ID is 1234 while my colleague's BuPa ID is 6789. The email I receive should have my BuPa ID at the end of the URL while the email my colleague receives should have their BuPa ID at the end of the URL (i.e.., my email would have this URL: ....example.com?id=1234 while my colleague would have this URL: ....example.com?id=6789).

In Content Studio, I built the email and followed this path to select the ID personalization: under Link Properties -> Personalized URL, selected "ID" (under the consumer level). I sent a test using "send using contact." The test send included all IDs (i.e., BuPa ID, email address, hashed, etc.) in the URL.

How do I place a rule in the Link Personalization to only pull through the BuPa ID origin?

Furthermore, if a recipient has 2 or more BuPa IDs, how do I limit the number of BuPa IDs passed through the URL?

In your response, will you please include screen shots as a way to confirm we're viewing the same platform settings/location/version?

If it helps, here is some information on which system I'm using:
Product Name: SAP Marketing Cloud 2502
Application ID: LAUNCHPAD
Application Version: 28.0.150

Thanks,
Daniella

entry.title: S4Hana Green field Implementation - Activate Methodology - Deliverables - 3 Pillars - Consultant POV
entry.content:
Intro:

Let us see the S/4 Hana Green field Implementation from Consultant point of view. This might help someone. SAP provides a lot of tools and instruction to implement a SAP Project like a software installation and initial setup in mobile. let's see.

Overview:

Before starting let us get to know about three pillars of SAP Activate.

  • SAP Best Practices
  • SAP Guided Configuration
  • SAP Activate methodology

1.1 SAP Best Practices: It is a set of standardized, pre-configured solutions made for different industries to implement SAP solutions. 

After buying SAP ERP license we should activate the best practice in SAP GUI via transaction /n/SMB/BBI. On activating this the basic configuration will be copied to new system. So that you can do copy the standard configs based on business and do hands-on with the test scripts which are available in Best practice explorer. 

Once implemented configuration in SIMG will appear. (OPJH)

On implementing this basic master data also will be copied to fresh SAP system for performing the test scripts which are available in Signavio (Previously SAP Best practice explorer)

1.2 Guided Configuration :  It's a set of tools, assets, and accelerators, designed for easy configuration of SAP systems and business processes. Using the SAP roadmap viewer, customers can identify which SAP Activate roadmap is needed, depending on the SAP solution in question.By exploring “SAP Endorsed Apps” you can find solutions such as the LeanIX EAM, which has integration with SAP Signavio.

Tools - Manage your Solution, Configure your solution apps in SAP Cloud edition will help on configuring the system like how we do initial setup after buying a mobile. 


SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) tools for extensibility is used to do customization in fiori apps (like hana studio or eclipse).

You can add SOLMAN (on-premise) and Cloud ALM tools as well which are used for task assignments, Defects monitoring, Project managements, and Creating Process flow docs.

Assets & Accelerators - SAP Signavio Process Navigator - It includes assets like best practices process flows, test documetns, templates, and documentation that provide guidance and support throughout the configuration process. Using this documents we can run the test scripts in SAP to get clear idea. Note: You need Suser id to download the test scripts.

Downloaded Test script contains data:

Master data in New SAP system after scope activation via /n/SMB/BBI:

Business roles needed will be added to system by scope activation in on premise.

In Cloud apps like Maintain Business Roles are available:

In Process flow diagram same roles can be seen on top 

1.3 Activate Methodology: Roadmap It's a project-implementation methodology used to plan and implement complex SAP solutions. It is built on the Agile methodology, which takes an iterative approach to continuously improve processes to increase the project’s overall quality and success. (Sucessor of ASAP). The 6 phases of Activate methodology are

Discover - > Prepare - > Explore - > Realize - > Deploy - > Run

Main Part (Based on Experience)

Discover phase

In the Discover phase customer’s core team is enabled to understand breadth, depth, and functionality of RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition. The team would learn the benefits and value the solution can bring to customers’ business.

Consultant Activities:

Here Sales team/Solution architects/Consultants will interact with the Business process owners (The person who closely works with materials in shopfloor to plan and produce it) and get a list of business process (Global Scope items - J44, BJ5 - MTS) that the business have. Sample  . Link

L1 - Level 1 L2L3L4ExplanationAvailability
      
ManufacturingProduction PlanningJ44 - Material Requirements Planning Main scenario This process enables your material requirements planning (MRP) department to automate the procurement planning. YES
ManufacturingProduction OperationBJ5 - Make-to-Stock Production - Discrete Manufacturing (OP) This scope item enables production planning in make-to-stock scenarios for a serial number managed finished good.NO

This list will be shared to Project Management team to write the contract, Cost, Resources, and so on. Implement only the necessary GSI on first live. If timeline is big consider all. If time is less consider less GSI. Otherwise you will not get time for documentation and pressure will be more. If you are new to this, then go through the GSI in Signavio, Download the test scripts and execute each business process to get idea. Collect all the GSI is worksheet and explain it to BPO to get a confirmation on it. Also customer will provided with demo system to understand the SAP.

Deliverable: Documentation to be given to client which got prepared by consultants or PM.

1.3.2 Prepare phase

The purpose of the Prepare phase is to provide the initial planning and preparation for the project. In this phase, the project is started, plans are finalized, project team is assigned, and work is under way to start the project optimally.

Consultant Activities: NA

But project management team will use the outputs from Discover phase to prepare a budget, timeline, policies and so on and give it to sales team. Later sales team in SAP Partner (Infosys, TCS) will sit with Client (Pepsi, Aramco) and finalize the agreement considering the cost, timeline, benefits and so on. Client will choose the SAP partner based on their wish. Once agreement got signed Service companies (Info, TCS) will post in LinkedIn that they won a project and start it.

Deliverable: PM, Client, Partner aggrements.

1.3.3 Explore phase - DEV

In this Explore phase the project team will conduct fit-to-standard workshops, verify, and confirm the to-be business processes with process models. Meanwhile master data and organizational requirements get identified. Organizational learning needs are identified and documented. After sign-off on delta requirements, design documents, and implementation plan the Q-gate from Explore to realize gets closed.

Consultant activities:

Prepare a PPT with process flow taken from .

PM will take consultants to client place and introduce them to BPO. Then arrange meetings between them to discuss about the GSI base on there timeline. Here Consultant should collect the master data from bpo to understand the business process. Using the BOM & Routing consultant should frame & map the process in SAP. Ofcourse bpo will take consultant to shopfloor for showcasing their prodution process. From ourside we need to categorize the mrp date for materials (Finished - MRP based, Raw - Consumption or time based) in our mind. Understand how many employee working as planners, supervisors, operators for material and capacity. Also we can frame SAP roles and authorization based on their activities. Find out the process which we cannot map it in standard and note it down as backlogs/ Custom objects. Prepare Functional specification document. Don't assume any fields by yourselves on preparing it. Get idea from BPO (from where the fields are to be fetched). Frame a business process flow for the client by taking reference from Signavio best practice explorer. Try to map the process on fit to standard basis.

Collect the key data structure information for doing the configurations.

MRP ControllerPlantMRP ControllerMRP controller name
 000101Cartridge Packing
 000102Cartridge Assembly

 

Order Types and Number RangeOrder TypeDescriptionNR FromNR To
 PP01Standard production order000001000000000001999999
 PP02Standard production order (external NA)1000000000002ZZZZZZZZZZZ

Get idea about there products and understand the list of MRP types that are used by them like CBP, MRP, TBP, and so on.

Convert the LTMC Excel template to easy layman format and share it with BPO to get the list of master data.

At last put all the collected information (Process flow, Roles, Backlogs, KDS)  in Solution design document

Deliverable:

Key Data Structure Signoff - Documents contains the key figures that are going to be configured in system.

FTS signoff - Document contain the process flow diagrams. Optional 

SDD signoff - Document contains the to be business process flow, steps, Backlogs, and Roles.

FRS Signoff - Functional requirment specification for backlogs.

TRS - Technical requirment specification for backlogs.

Note: Signoff documents to be verified by BPO, PM, Consultants and signed.

1.3.4 Realize phase - DEV - QAS

Project management joint with workstreams will plan sprints. Project team focuses on implementing defined solution using incremental build iterations. It will configure, extend, integrate, test, confirm, and document the entire end-to-end solution and prepare for legacy data conversion. The project team actively works with business representatives to ensure a good fit of the built solution to the requirements from the backlog.

The team executes build cycles called ‘Sprints’ to build and test the functionality. The team will conduct walkthroughs with the business users within each sprint to provide frequent checkpoints that the build is meeting the requirements. And will go through multiple iterations to develop functionality that is then ready to be released to production. For each release, the team conducts full end-to-end testing. The project team releases results of multiple iterations to the business users to accelerate time to value and provide early access to finalized functionality. Each sprint release is thoroughly tested in the end-to-end integration test and the user acceptance test.

Consultant Activities:

Do Configuration in the system based on the KDS document in DEV. Maintain some master data which you collected from business during explore phase. Do the WRICEF objects against each GSI in DEV. Later move all to QAS. Do all integration testing with other modules in QAS.

Run through the business GSI process with new custom configurations and WRICEF (backlogs) to BPO.

Collect the changes and correct it and show it on next GSI sessions.

After all session, give the User test scripts to BPO for testing and training for testing the same.

Provide user manuals.

Deliverable:

Configuration Files: Document contains Module wise SIMG/SPRO configurations

Solution walk through PPT: 

User acceptance testing Signoff Excel: Test scripts contains test values which operator do with the WRICEF.

User Manuals Word: Document contains step by step guide with screenshots for user reference in future.

1.3.5 Deploy phase - PRD

In the Deploy phase, the team makes sure that the business is ready to perform the cutover activities. The complexity of these activities will depend on various factors including the number of users that are impacted by the solution and the business scope of the deployment. Teams may need to conduct multiple cutover simulations as part of the preparation for go-live. Once the solution is live, the project team provides a defined period of post go-live support (hyper-care period). The project is then formally closed, and the solution is now in a separate PRODUCTIVE OPERATIONS or RUN phase

Consultant Activities:

After UAT acceptance signoff move the configuration & WRICEF from QAS to PRD. Find out the report in SAP which can be used to verify the count of master data. Share the collected master data with bpo and get it verified. After confirmation upload it to system. Later share SAP reports with the bpo and get it verified. Find out the open orders, Transactional data which need to be moved to new system. Close it in old system if possible. Move only the orders which will run for a long while during the blackout period. Get confirmation for that also. Before uploading the open orders use custom external number range and upload it with old legasy order numbers. Later we can change the NR. Also before uploading order sort in ascending. After uploading get that also verified with BPO. After golive anouncement on golive day, sit with Operators in shopfloor to complete the transactions (PO creation, Confirmation, GR posting) in PRD system.  

Deliverable:

Master data Validation & Transaction execution signoff.

1.3.6 Run phase​

Customer is live, with this begins the continuous learning process, that includes validation of newly released features and value assessment of business relevant innovations against current solution.

Behind this high-level phase descriptions, SAP Activate Methodology provides helpful details for deliverables and tasks as well as more sources to relevant information.

Consultant Activities:

Monitor the system and Identify innovation opportunities and implement it. 

Deliverable:

Change documents.

Conclusion:

I hope this may help you on your implementation project. Please comment if there are changes. Have a look to Link

Restriction

entry.title: Como me preparei para a Certificação SAP LeanIX Enterprise Architecture
entry.content:
Lançada em Julho de 2024, a SAP Certified Associate Enterprise Architecture Consultant - SAP LeanIX, é uma forma de validar que você possui habilidades e conhecimentos em Arquitetura Corporativa usando a solução LeanIX. Ela prova que você é capaz de conduzir um projeto de implementação dessa solução.

Link para acessar essa certificação => AQUI

Antes falar sobre o processo de preparação, gostaria de contextualizar dois pontos importantes.

O que é LeanIX?

SAP LeanIX é líder de mercado em arquitetura empresarial, impulsionando a modernização dos cenários de TI e a transformação contínua dos negócios. Proporcionando uma abordagem automatizada e baseada em dados aprimorados com IA, o SAP LeanIX ajuda as organizações a tomar decisões acertadas e a colaborar de forma eficaz.

Fundada em 2012, em Bonn, na Alemanha, foi adquirida pela SAP em Outubro de 2023, e desde então tem sido considerada como extremamente relevante dentro da estratégia SAP no apoio das jornadas de migração para a nuvem em seus clientes.

 
O que é arquitetura corporativa (Enterprise Architecture)?

A arquitetura empresarial (EA) é um modelo conceitual que define a estrutura e a operação das organizações. A sua intenção é determinar como uma organização pode atingir efetivamente seus objetivos atuais e futuros, por meio da prática de análise, planejamento, projeto e execução.

A arquitetura empresarial ajuda as empresas a passar pela jornada de transformação, uma vez que se concentra em reunir aplicações e processos de negócios, com o objetivo de construir um ambiente perfeito. A utilização de estruturas de EA aumentou em resposta ao aumento das tecnologias empresariais durante a década de 1980, quando a necessidade de uma forma de responder ao rápido crescimento tecnológico era parte integrante da estratégia empresarial. Posteriormente, esse processo se expandiu para toda a empresa, não apenas para a TI. Dessa forma, o restante do negócio teria a garantia de estar alinhado com a transformação tecnológica.

Os conceitos de arquitetura empresarial são variáveis, portanto não serão iguais para cada organização. Diferentes partes de uma organização também podem ver a EA de forma diferente. Por exemplo, programadores e outros profissionais técnicos de TI consideram as estratégias de arquitetura empresarial em termos de infraestrutura, aplicativos e componentes de gerenciamento sob seu controle. No entanto, os arquitetos empresariais ainda são responsáveis pela execução da análise da estrutura empresarial, bem como o suporte da tecnologia necessário.

 

Dito isso, vou compartilhar os principais pontos de me ajudaram na preparação para essa certificação.

Curso SAP LeanIX

No meu caso, o primeiro passo foi realizar o curso disponível no Learning da SAP. Particularmente, gostei bastante da forma como o conteúdo é apresentado, mesclando entre vídeos, conteúdos escritos e exemplos práticos.

Um ponto importante é o foco no conteúdo. Para mim o que funcionou bem foi estudar esse conteúdo no início do dia, antes de começar minha rotina de trabalho. E mesmo assim, optei por desativar as notificações no celular e computador, para realmente mergulhar no aprendizado.

Nesse curso você terá o acesso ao Workspace de Demo para ter uma experiência muito interessante com a solução.

Link para o Curso da SAP => AQUI

Documentação

Uma ótima fonte para aprofundar no conhecimento da solução, é o estudo das documentações disponíveis. São materiais muito didáticos e com exemplos das

O Conteúdo é disposto entre os seguintes temas: Onboarding, Metamodelo, Casos de uso, Guia do usuário, Integrações e documentação de desenvolvimento (esse último não é relevante para a certificação).

Link para a documentação => AQUI

Vídeos

Para auxiliar no processo de entendimento da solução, preparei uma série de vídeos explicando em detalhes as principais funções do LeanIX:

O que é Arquitetura Corporativa com LeanIX?- https://abrir.link/QduRi

Conhecendo a solução da SAP LeanIX - https://abrir.link/EfuZk

A estrutura do Metamodelo LeanIX - https://abrir.link/FVjCK

Alinhamento entre áreas de Negócios e suas Aplicações - https://abrir.link/eRAPb

O Inventário de informações no LeanIX - https://abrir.link/yRTOu

Ferramentas de colaboração - https://abrir.link/FJsmo

Gestão do portifólio de Aplicações na sua arquitetura - https://abrir.link/wZtao

Matriz de Aplicações x Atividades de negócio - https://abrir.link/ioCuS

Comunidade SAP LeanIX

Outra forma muito interessante de aprender sobre LeanIX e se tornar um membro da comunidade. Pois além de ter ótimos conteúdos publicados por especialistas do mundo todo, você ainda pode tirar dúvidas ou compartilhar os seus desafios com a solução.

Link para a comunidade => AQUI

Compilação do conteúdo

Como se pode ver, são muitas as fontes valiosas de informações para essa preparação. Por isso, é importante organizar a suas anotações com os principais pontos de aprendizado, isso da forma que você achar melhor (eu utilizei o OneNote).

Alguns pontos importantes sobre o dia do exame

  • Organize-se para estar preparado para a prova com 30 minutos de antecedência, para evitar que algum contratempo impacte a sua pontualidade.
  • Valide todos os requerimentos técnicos em seu computador (ao realizar o seu agendamento, você vai receber um email com os principais pontos de verificação). Se possível, prepare uma redundância para o caso de ter problema com energia elétrica ou internet durante a prova.
  • Tenha dois documentos com foto que comprovem a sua identidade (confirme a data de validade!).
  • Prepare bem o local onde você irá realizar a prova. Importante que não tenham outros computadores, tablets, celulares, nem mesmo papeis com anotações, espelhos ou qualquer coisa que possa parecer um facilitador para obter informações.
  • Reserve um cômodo sem fluxo de pessoas (lembre-se que nenhuma pessoa poderá acessar o local durante a prova).
  • Procure manter o controle emocional e evite alimentos pesados no dia da prova.

Desejo muito sucesso em sua jornada rumo a essa importante certificação!

Caso necessite de alguma ajuda adicional, me avise!

entry.title: #SITCPS2025 - 🗣️ Uso de APIs na Comunicação Bancária dos Processos Financeiros no SAP
entry.content:
Palestrante: Manuel Robalinho @MRobalinho 
Biografia: Consultor SAP certificado com 25 anos de experiência internacional em projetos no Brasil, Portugal e Angola. É mestre em Ciência da Computação, doutorando na Universidade de Coimbra e SAP Champion. Organizador dos eventos SAP Inside Track em Fortaleza e Porto.
Título da Palestra: Uso de APIs na Comunicação Bancária dos Processos Financeiros no SAP
Descrição da Palestra: A apresentação explora como as APIs podem otimizar a comunicação bancária em processos financeiros no SAP, abrangendo contas a pagar, contas a receber, extratos bancários, atualização de moedas e integração bancária. Destaca-se o uso de APIs para aumentar a segurança, eficiência e inovação nos processos financeiros.

Integração de APIs no SAP S/4HANA: Manuel Robalinho destaca a importância das APIs como facilitadoras da comunicação entre o SAP S/4HANA e sistemas bancários externos. As APIs permitem a automação de processos como a execução de pagamentos (F110) e o processamento de extratos bancários (FF.5), resultando em maior eficiência e redução de erros.LinkedIn

Automatização de Processos Financeiros: A palestra aborda como as APIs automatizam tarefas críticas, como a atualização de saldos bancários e a conciliação de contas, proporcionando dados em tempo real e melhorando a tomada de decisões financeiras.

Segurança na Troca de Informações: Robalinho enfatiza o uso de certificados digitais e protocolos de criptografia nas APIs para garantir a segurança na troca de informações sensíveis entre o SAP e os bancos, atendendo às exigências de compliance e proteção de dados.

Desafios na Implementação: A apresentação reconhece que nem todos os bancos oferecem APIs completas, o que pode exigir a manutenção de métodos tradicionais de integração, como layouts CNAB, em certos casos.SAP Community

Benefícios Estratégicos: A adoção de APIs no SAP proporciona benefícios como maior agilidade nos processos financeiros, redução de custos operacionais e capacidade de adaptação rápida às mudanças regulatórias e de mercado.

Conclusão: A palestra de Manuel Robalinho oferece uma visão abrangente sobre como as APIs transformam a comunicação bancária no SAP, destacando tanto os benefícios quanto os desafios dessa integração.

entry.title: Re: Streamline asset inspection process with Visual Asset Health Monitoring
entry.content:
This looks to be the "slider" at the top of an electric train/tram where it contacts to the power line as the train moves.   What then seems to be missing is a linking of the specific train to the slider in the image....unless there is an identifier (barcode) that would also show in the picture.

Here then we have the need for 2 data captures to properly identify the maintenance item / technical object.   This would be a common use case for a robot dog doing visual inspections in its wandering, or a drone flight -- that the identification of the item is needed to then link to the measurement found.   With the 2 data elements, then there is the opportunity to have the images come from any source, it just has to know which one.  You then also can match the measurement type to the object being observed.   For example
(1) a picture shows a train ID, and then the slider -- so the thickness needs to be interpreted.
(2) a picture shows a Pump ID and then a view of the Oil Reservoir - so it needs to provide a level measurement.

The missing information then is also informative...for which trains have I NOT measured the slider thickness in the past 7 days?

entry.title: #SITCPS2025 - 🗣️ Abertura do Evento
entry.content:
Contextualizou o propósito institucional da FACAMP, abordando sua missão de formar líderes conscientes e capacitados para os desafios do mercado atual e futuro. Sabatini compartilhou reflexões sobre educação, tecnologia e sociedade, reforçando a importância da formação interdisciplinar e humanista.

 

  • Educação com propósito e acolhimento
    Sabatini iniciou dando as boas-vindas aos participantes, destacando o prazer em acolhê-los na FACAMP. Reforçou que a instituição rejeita o modelo de "torre de marfim" universitária, promovendo uma formação conectada à realidade e ao mercado.

  • Visão histórica da tecnologia
    Compartilhou suas experiências pessoais com a internet nos anos 1990, enfatizando como as previsões tecnológicas se concretizaram e como isso reforça a necessidade de preparar os alunos para um futuro de mudanças constantes.

  • Crítica ao modelo educacional tradicional
    Sabatini argumentou contra a simples transmissão de conteúdo e defendeu o aprendizado por meio do debate e da interação – modelo herdado de Sócrates e Platão – como a verdadeira revolução educacional.

  • Multidisciplinaridade e pensamento crítico
    A FACAMP promove uma formação que vai além da técnica, integrando direito, história, línguas e tecnologia em todos os cursos. Isso desenvolve profissionais aptos a pensar criticamente e resolver problemas complexos.

  • Responsabilidade social na elite educacional
    Reconheceu que os alunos da instituição são privilegiados por poderem estudar em tempo integral sem necessidade de trabalhar. Contudo, essa condição carrega a responsabilidade de contribuir para um Brasil mais igualitário e justo.

  • Mercado e empregabilidade de alto nível
    34% dos egressos da FACAMP assumem cargos de liderança em até cinco anos de carreira. A formação foca em preparar os alunos para atuar em empresas de alta performance e liderar transformações corporativas e sociais.

  • Facampetos e parcerias corporativas
    A palestra também apresentou o núcleo Facampetos, que articula ações como branding no campus, cursos customizados e projetos de inteligência aplicada em parceria com empresas como a SAP.

  • Formação integral e soft skills avançadas
    A formação proposta visa desenvolver habilidades técnicas e também competências interpessoais e analíticas essenciais para os desafios do futuro do trabalho.

  • Customização e inovação em pós-graduação
    A FACAMP aposta em cursos curtos e mão na massa, abandonando o modelo tradicional de pós-graduação em prol de experiências práticas e relevantes para o mercado.

  • Conexão entre academia e empresas
    Sabatini ressaltou que a academia deve estar em constante diálogo com a sociedade e o setor produtivo, criando soluções reais e duradouras para os desafios contemporâneos.

entry.title: #SITCPS2025 - 🗣️ Estudo de Caso
entry.content:
Marcelo Cabreira, executivo da Accentory, com 20 anos de experiência e atuação internacional. É pesquisador de mestrado na Unicamp com foco em arquitetura corporativa aplicada a projetos SAP.

Título da Palestra:
"Abordagem Estratégica de Arquitetura para Projetos SAP"

Descrição:
Nesta apresentação de 15 minutos, Marcelo compartilhou sua visão sobre como a arquitetura deve ser entendida de forma estratégica, baseada em pesquisa acadêmica e estudos de caso reais. A palestra abordou como integrar valor de negócio, governança e redução de custos com uma abordagem arquitetural sólida.

 

Resumo

  • Introdução Estratégica à Arquitetura:
    Marcelo propôs que a arquitetura para projetos SAP vá além do técnico, sendo um elemento estratégico essencial. Com base em sua pesquisa de mestrado, destacou a importância de alinhar arquitetura com os objetivos do negócio para entregar valor e ROI.

  • Panorama da Pesquisa Científica:
    Foram analisados 2000 artigos científicos, dos quais apenas 30 foram considerados relevantes. Esse filtro rigoroso mostrou como a abordagem estratégica de arquitetura ainda é pouco difundida, especialmente em mercados como o brasileiro.

  • Estudo de Caso Aplicado:
    Marcelo apresentou um estudo de caso desenvolvido desde 2019, validado por profissionais da Accent. Um dos exemplos demonstrou uma redução de horas estimadas em um projeto SAP de 40 mil para 15 mil horas, graças à atuação antecipada da arquitetura corporativa.

  • Nove Estágios da Transformação Arquitetural:
    A jornada desde o business case até a entrega técnica envolve nove estágios distintos. Os três primeiros são estratégicos e incluem evangelização, alinhamento e construção de valor percebido para os stakeholders.

  • Crítica ao Pragmatismo na TI:
    Foi destacado que desde os anos 1970, a TI sofre com a falta de pragmatismo. Marcelo argumenta que uma arquitetura bem fundamentada é a chave para evitar erros repetidos e eliminar crenças em soluções milagrosas.

  • Validação Acadêmica e Profissional:
    A metodologia incluiu revisão bibliográfica e aplicação de surveys com profissionais de TI. A análise evidenciou que muitos ainda veem a arquitetura como uma “torre de marfim”, distante da realidade prática.

  • Formação de Arquitetos Corporativos:
    A Accent tem investido na formação de arquitetos com visão holística, capazes de dialogar com todas as camadas organizacionais e entregar soluções integradas, robustas e economicamente viáveis.

  • Dificuldades de Alinhamento e Viés Cognitivo:
    Marcelo comentou como o viés da formação de cada arquiteto afeta o design arquitetural e reforçou que é necessário trabalhar o alinhamento entre áreas técnicas e de negócio como prioridade essencial.

  • Resultados Reais e Casos de Sucesso:
    Com exemplos de grandes empresas como AB InBev e Braskem, Marcelo reforçou que os resultados comprovam a eficácia de uma arquitetura voltada para resultados financeiros e valor estratégico.

  • Encerramento com Perspectiva Humana:
    A palestra foi encerrada com convites para conversas no stand e uma fala direta sobre a importância do arquiteto corporativo como agente de mudança, agregador de valor e portador de más notícias quando necessário.

entry.title: Google Tag Manager Configuration
entry.content:
My client sent me code to add to Career Site Builder for Google Tag Manager.  They sent two snippets of code with the following instructions for each code snippet:

  1. Code Snippet 1: "Copy and paste this snippet in the code of every page of your website, immediately after the <head> element. Don’t add more than one Google tag to each page."
  2. Code Snippet 2, which is supposed to be used for the Application Thank You Page that the candidate sees after they apply to a position on RMK: "Paste the below snippet in between the <head></head> tags of the page(s) you'd like to track, right after the Google tag."

From what I understand for Google Tag Manager and CSB, all we need is the Google Tag Manager ID and we add that to the Google Tag Manager ID field in CSB -> Site Configuration -> Google Tag Manager ID field.  Then we flip the the "Use Google Tag Manager in Career Site Wrapper" toggle to Yes, which adds the Google Tag Manager to the Application Thank You Page as well.  

I have added the Google Tag Manager ID to Career Site Builder for the client and flipped the the "Use Google Tag Manager in Career Site Wrapper" toggle to Yes, but the client is saying that the Google tag and conversion pixel are still reading as inactive.  

A couple of questions:

  1. Am I even able to add the code snippets that the client provided into RMK/RCM/SuccessFactors in accordance with how the client instructed (described above)?  I am thinking I could add a Custom Component to each page, but I don't think that is going to place the code where they are instructing to place it on the CSB pages..........so, I don't think we can and this would not be an option, right?
  2. If using the code provided is an option or something that I need to add to CSB for this to work, then how do I go about adding Code Snippet 1 to CSB in accordance with the instructions ("Copy and paste it in the code of every page of your website, immediately after the <head> element. Don’t add more than one Google tag to each page.")?  Is it a custom component on each page?  Is it added to CSB -> Appearance -> Global -> Java Script?  
  3. If using the code provided by the client is an option or something that I need to add to RCM for this to work, then how do I go about adding Code Snippet 2 to the Application Thank You page in accordance with the instructions ("Paste the below snippet in between the <head></head> tags of the page(s) you'd like to track, right after the Google tag.").  I have tried to add this to the Application Thank You screen in Manage Recruiting Settings (similar to what we would do for a pixel), but the code does not save.  

Thank you,

Joe Burkart

entry.title: Field Description and Usage in Warehouse Process Type Setup in SAP EWM
entry.content:
Introduction: This document offers a concise and practical guide to configuring Warehouse Process Types (WPT) in SAP EWM. It highlights essential settings like process categories, priorities, warehouse order rules, and control indicators that drive efficient execution of inbound, outbound, and internal warehouse tasks.

      

The Warehouse Process Type in SAP EWM is structured around six key configuration areas, each playing a critical role in defining how warehouse tasks are executed:

  • Warehouse Process Type Definition
  • Control Parameters for Putaway and Stock Removal
  • Settings for Storage Process 
  • Settings for  Change Configuration
  • Settings for Production Supply 
  • Settings for Warehouse Request 

Below is a detailed explanation of each configuration area and the functional significance of each field.

                                                   Warehouse Process Type:

The Warehouse Process Category in SAP EWM defines the type of logistical operation to be executed—such as Putaway, stock removal, or internal movements. It plays a critical role in determining how the system creates, processes, and confirms warehouse tasks within various business processes.

Putaway:
Facilitates the movement of stock from the staging or goods receipt (GR) area into designated warehouse storage bins.

Stock Removal:
Represents the picking process, where stock is retrieved from warehouse bins to fulfill outbound deliveries or consumption.

Internal Warehouse Movement:
Covers internal stock transfers, such as relocating goods from one storage bin to another within the warehouse.

Posting Change:
Used for updating stock attributes, including changes in quality status, batch classification, or ownership without physical movement.

  • CLSP (Crossline Stock Putaway):
    Manages consolidated putaway tasks involving multiple delivery lines or handling units (HUs).
  • INTL (Internal Movement):
    Used for internal stock transfers, such as bin-to-bin or area-to-area movements within the warehouse.
  • INVE (Physical Inventory):
    Facilitates inventory counting processes as part of periodic or ad hoc stock-taking activities.
  • PICK (Stock Removal):
    Directs picking operations from storage bins to fulfill outbound deliveries or production consumption.
  • PTWY (Putaway):
    Governs the placement of received goods into designated storage bins.
  • REPL (Replenishment):
    Initiates stock replenishment for picking areas to ensure continuous material availability.
  • STCH (Posting Change):
    Supports updates to stock attributes—such as quality status or ownership—without physical relocation.

The Warehouse Order Creation Rule (WO Rule) in the Warehouse Process Type defines how warehouse tasks are bundled into executable warehouse orders. In complex processes like POSC, the WO Rule may also incorporate storage process steps to guide task creation and sequence. This ensures efficient task execution by controlling how and when WTs are grouped based on parameters like activity, quantity, or weight.

                                              Control for Putaway/Stock Removal:

                                                      Settings for Storage Process:

                                    Settings for Posting Change and Production Supply:

                                                Settings for Warehouse Requests:

 

Conclusion:
A clear understanding of Warehouse Process Types and their configuration is critical to optimizing task execution in SAP EWM. When properly set up, these settings enhance operational efficiency, improve resource utilization, and ensure precise inventory management. This guide serves as a valuable reference for implementation and support consultants navigating real-world EWM project scenarios.

entry.title: Re: BSP future in Private Cloud 2023 FPS2
entry.content:
Most likely the announcement that BSP is obsolete and should not be used anymore was posted in SDN back in 2006. After 2 decades and I don't know how many migrations on the community you might have a  hard time finding how many times this discussion have happened.

Despite exceptions and SAP having a hard time to say that a technology is dead, Web Dynpro was the replacement of BSP. Web Dynpro in 2025 is as old as drinking wine. SAPUI5 was the de facto replacement of Web Dynpro.

That does not mean SAP has tried to rewrite BSP applications into Web Dynpro Applications nor it has tried to rewrite Web Dynpro apps to SAPUI5 applications nor it will somehow rewrite all SAPUI5 freestyle apps with Fiori elements or SAP Build Apps. That does not even make sense. ABAP reports and module pools are still there and SAP found a way to make them accessible in Fiori other than rewriting those apps from scratch.

In other words, when you say:

but our organisation has been given specific guidance that BSPs will no longer be supported in S/4HANA

Your organization should not be creating BSP applications for almost 2 decades right now and that decision should have been taken way before S/4HANA was created in 2015.

Maybe you did not mention some key information here. if your context is in SAP CRM and you are actually referring to CRM Web UI, than I can agree that you might have reasons for still using it despite other new technologies were introduced over the years. Still Fiori/UI5 was the right way to go for at least 1 decade now.

Maybe there is some misunderstanding here when you wrote:

Now, I know that BSPs deployed in our PoC S/4HANA environment function perfectly fine

If you mean a SAPUI5 application which is deployed to an on-premise system and which becomes visible in SE80 by using the "BSP Application" option - then your question is not about BSP at all.

Just to be very clear - this is how BSP code looks like. It's a mix of HTML code together with ABAP code.

<html>
  <body>
    <h2>Flight List (from SFLIGHT)</h2>

    <table border="1">
      <tr>
        <th>Carrier</th>
        <th>Connection</th>
        <th>Flight Date</th>
      </tr>

      <% 
        DATA: lt_flights TYPE TABLE OF sflight,
              ls_flight TYPE sflight.

        SELECT * FROM sflight INTO TABLE lt_flights UP TO 10 ROWS.

        LOOP AT lt_flights INTO ls_flight.
      %>
        <tr>
          <td><%= ls_flight-carrid %></td>
          <td><%= ls_flight-connid %></td>
          <td><%= ls_flight-fldate %></td>
        </tr>
      <%
        ENDLOOP.
      %>
    </table>
  </body>
</html>

BSP applications were not "deployed" because they were literally created in SE80 itself.

If that is the case the one to blame is the person who had the idea to reuse existing functionality in SE80 to display SAPUI5 applications just because they are also web applications. Really confusing but that does not mean SAPUI5 apps are BSP apps.

Please clarify this a little bit more if needed.

entry.title: Re: material not maintained by purchasing

Currently, the client enters the Transporter ID manually in Invoice . However, the client would like this field to be available directly in the Manage Billing Document screen for specific customers, so they do not have to enter it manually each time.

entry.title: Re: Date and Time in SAP Gateway Foundation
entry.content:
I find Unix Epoch datetimes insufferable and annoying when UTC ISO 8601 format passed as string can easily be consumed by virtually the entire world, and converted easily in ABAP. 

This routine will turn a UTC timestamp into UTC ISO 8601 format with explicit time zone specification (ex: 2025-05-16T19:52:30Z)
lv_datetime = cl_xlf_date_time=>create( lv_timestamp ).

Then this one will parse an incoming UTC ISO 8601 datetime stamp back into UTC timestamp:
DATA(lv_timestamp) = cl_xlf_date_time=>parse( lv_datetime ). 

This was born out of us probably misunderstanding how the heck the dateTime in oData 2.0 was supposed to work initially many years ago, but lead us to something easily understandable by humans when viewing a JSON payload and interoperable with the rest of the world, unlike the pointlessly frustrating Unix Epoch time. 

Anyone else in this boat? ISO 8601 for life!

entry.title: Re: IRPA Add Shedule Trigger Cloud Stuio
entry.content:
You could go with one of the following options:

1. Instead of one trigger, create a trigger for each month, and schedule to execute once every 12 months, or once a year.

2. Instead of scheduled triggers, create an API based trigger. Run a daily script in either powershell or some similar script which runs locally on the machine, check the current date and if it is the last date of the month, then call the API trigger that starts the automation.

Do let me know in case you find any other convenient solution or this.

Best Regards,

Rohit

entry.title: Re: RF verification profile in S4 Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Emmanuel,

Thanks for your question.

  1. Bin Verification Behaviour
    In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the system automatically expects source and destination bin verification when you include both source HU and destination bin in the RF verification profile, even if the source bin is not explicitly set. This is standard behaviour to ensure traceability and accurate goods movement.
  2. Quantity Field Limitation
    Currently, the RF interface provides only one quantity field, which is used to verify the movement quantity. Separate source/destination quantity checks aren't available via standard functionality.

Suggested Actions:

  • Review and adjust the assigned RF verification profile to suit your process needs.

Thank you and kind regards

Chuma
